title: eriogonum divaricatum
intro: Eriogonum divaricatum, also known as Mormon tea, is a type of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. It has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, with its health benefits including its potential to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote overall well-being. The plant can be prepared as a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it can also be dried and used as a tincture or added to herbal supplements.
